summary: Gruber follows up on his earlier report that BBEdit Lite was no longer available, noting that Bare Bones Software has clarified their policy. While BBEdit Lite is discontinued and unsupported, it remains legally available for download. Gruber documents the exact change in wording on Bare Bones' website to show he wasn't wrong in his original reading — the page initially said BBEdit Lite was 'no longer available,' which was later softened. He also notes a new comparison page between TextWrangler and BBEdit Lite.
key_points: Bare Bones Software reversed or clarified their position on BBEdit Lite's availability after initially stating it was 'no longer available'
Gruber documents the exact before-and-after wording change on the BBEdit Lite web page to vindicate his earlier eulogy
BBEdit Lite remains legally downloadable, with versions going back to 3.5 for System 6 still on the FTP site
A new comparison page was created to show differences between TextWrangler and BBEdit Lite
money_quotes: Ends up this comment from my initial look at TextWrangler is no longer true.

Just to prove I wasn't smoking crack when I wrote the above eulogy for BBEdit Lite, a comparison between yesterday's update to the BBEdit Lite web page and the version in my local cache from a few days ago shows the difference.
Version 6.1.2 is still available for download on their FTP site, as are versions 4.6, 4.1, and even version 3.5, for you holdouts still clinging to System 6.
key_insight: Gruber meticulously documents a subtle policy reversal by Bare Bones Software, demonstrating his characteristic attention to exact wording and his insistence on getting the details right.
